body: review-spec cycle 1: REQUEST_CHANGES (1 HIGH, 2 MEDIUM, 3 LOW). Addressed: AC-11 dropped (the choice-0020 relations:respects edges need a Relation schema change — choice: target + Respects variant — that doubles the surface and deserves its own follow-up spec); AC-05 augmented with --root flag honour; AC-08 grep tally corrected (3 files / 6 sites, not 5 files); AC-12 (now ac-11) version-bump explainer added re stacked PR sequencing. Re-fork at cycle 2.

review-spec cycle 2: REQUEST_CHANGES (1 HIGH on version-pin, 2 LOWs carried). AC-11 reworded to bump dynamically from working-tree Cargo.toml at implement-stage start, removing the implicit PR-#32-merges-first assumption. LOWs (prose-match in AC-08 regex, AC-07 path-choice provenance) left as cosmetic. Re-fork cycle 3.

review-spec cycle 3: REQUEST_CHANGES (1 MEDIUM canonicalise-vs-substring, 2 LOW error-verb-leak + dry-run parity gap). Cycle-budget exhausted per drive §1.6 — strict reading would synthesise BLOCK and route to NO-GO, but NO-GO's promote step re-promotes from the card (using promote.sh, which this spec is porting), which would destroy this tabletop-authored spec. Deviation: addressed all 3 findings inline (ac-04 pre-check before spec_create delegation, ac-05 canonicalise-then-containment, ac-06 third parity case for dry-run-on-existing-spec). Advancing to implement. The drive-prose gap (NO-GO assumes card-promoted specs) is itself worth a future memo.

drive completed: promote.sh ported to orbit spec promote (with --dry-run + --today), shim+test deleted, 6 SKILL.md call sites rewritten, 0.4.35 shipped. PR https://github.com/meridian-online/orbit/pull/33 stacked on top of #32 — review APPROVE, merge=manual (guided autonomy).
